answer:
**1. In-Depth User Understanding:** Recognizing the human inclination to seek protection through misguided means, authentic narrations address the spiritual journey of the believers by explicitly discouraging superstitious practices. True believers are described as those who do not practice charms, take omens, or use cauterization, but instead repose their absolute trust in their Lord [1, 2].

**2. Robust and Enduring Framework:** The prohibition of relying on amulets is built upon the steadfast theological foundation that all benefit and harm originate exclusively from Allah. Surah Al-An'am 6:17 establishes this enduring reality by declaring that if God touches someone with adversity, no one can remove it except Him, and if He touches someone with good, He alone is competent over all things [3].

**3. Elegant and Lucid Presentation:** The Quran eloquently clarifies the complete powerlessness of any object or entity relied upon besides God. Surah Az-Zumar 39:38 presents a lucid argument by asking whether anything invoked besides God could possibly remove His intended harm or withhold His intended mercy [4]. 

**4. Seamless and Accessible Experience:** This divine guidance provides a straightforward and highly accessible path by directing the believer's complete reliance toward the Creator. As Surah Az-Zumar 39:38 concludes, believers are provided a simple, comforting truth to live by: "Sufficient for me is God; upon Him [alone] rely the [wise] reliers," thus eliminating the need for any charms or amulets [4].

references.cited_text: Book 001, Number 0423: 'Imran b. Husain reported: Verily the Messenger of Allah (may peace be upon him) said: Seventy

saw a star shooting last night? I said: It was I; then I said: I was in fact not (busy) in prayer, but was stung by a scorpion (and that is the reason why I was awake and had a glimpse of the shooting star). He said: Then what did you do? I said: I practised charm. He said: What urged you to do this? I said: (I did this according to the implied suggestion) of the hadith which al-Shu'ba narrated. He said: What did al-Shu'ba narrate to you? I said: Buraida b. Husaib al-Aslami narrated to us. The charm is of no avail except in case of the (evil influence) of an eye or the sting of a scorpion. He said: He who acted according to what he had heard (from the Holy Prophet) acted rightly, but Ibn 'Abbas narrated to us from the Apostle of Allah (may peace be upon him) that he said: There were brought before me the peoples and I saw an apostle and a small group (of his followers) along with him, another (apost-le) and one or two persons (along with him) and (still another) apostle having no one with him. When a very large group was brought to me I conceived as if it were my Ummah. Then it was said to me: It is Moses and his people. You should look at the horizon, and I saw a very huge group. It was again said to me: See the other side of the horizon, and there was (also) a very huge group. It was said to me: This is your Ummah, and amongst them there were seventy thousand persons who would be made to enter Paradise without rendering any account and without (suffering) any torment. He then stood up and went to his house. Then the people began to talk about the people who would be admitted to Paradise without rendering any account and without (suffering) any torment. Some of them said: They may be those who (have had the good fortune of living) in the company of the Mes-senger of Allah (may peace be upon him) and some of them said: They be those who were born in Is-lam and did not associate anything with Allah. Some people mentioned other things.  Thereupon came forth the Messenger of Allah (may peace be upon him) before them and he said: What was that which you were talking about? They informed him. He said: They are those persons who neither practise charm, nor ask others to practise it, nor do they take omens, and repose their trust in their Lord. Upon this 'Ukkasha b. Mihsan stood up and said: Supplicate for me that He should make me one among them. Upon this he (Messenger of Allah) said: Thou are one among them. Then another man stood up and said: Supplicate before Allah that He should make me one among them. Upon this he said: 'Ukkisha has preceded you.

It was again said to me: See the other side of the horizon, and there was (also) a very huge group. It was said to me: This is your Ummah, and amongst them there were seventy thousand persons who would be made to enter Paradise without rendering any account and without (suffering) any torment. He then stood up and went to his house. Then the people began to talk about the people who would be admitted to Paradise without rendering any account and without (suffering) any torment. Some of them said: They may be those who (have had the good fortune of living) in the company of the Mes-senger of Allah (may peace be upon him) and some of them said: They be those who were born in Is-lam and did not associate anything with Allah. Some people mentioned other things.  Thereupon came forth the Messenger of Allah (may peace be upon him) before them and he said: What was that which you were talking about? They informed him. He said: They are those persons who neither practise charm, nor ask others to practise it, nor do they take omens, and repose their trust in their Lord. Upon this 'Ukkasha b. Mihsan stood up and said: Supplicate for me that He should make me one among them. Upon this he (Messenger of Allah) said: Thou are one among them. Then another man stood up and said: Supplicate before Allah that He should make me one among them. Upon this he said: 'Ukkisha has preceded you.
my Lord, the punishment of a tre-mendous Day.”
38. And if you asked them, “Who created
Book 001, Number 0423: 'Imran b. Husain reported: Verily the Messenger of Allah (may peace be upon him) said: Seventy

saw a star shooting last night? I said: It was I; then I said: I was in fact not (busy) in prayer, but was stung by a scorpion (and that is the reason why I was awake and had a glimpse of the shooting star). He said: Then what did you do? I said: I practised charm. He said: What urged you to do this? I said: (I did this according to the implied suggestion) of the hadith which al-Shu'ba narrated. He said: What did al-Shu'ba narrate to you? I said: Buraida b. Husaib al-Aslami narrated to us. The charm is of no avail except in case of the (evil influence) of an eye or the sting of a scorpion. He said: He who acted according to what he had heard (from the Holy Prophet) acted rightly, but Ibn 'Abbas narrated to us from the Apostle of Allah (may peace be upon him) that he said: There were brought before me the peoples and I saw an apostle and a small group (of his followers) along with him, another (apost-le) and one or two persons (along with him) and (still another) apostle having no one with him. When a very large group was brought to me I conceived as if it were my Ummah. Then it was said to me: It is Moses and his people. You should look at the horizon, and I saw a very huge group. It was again said to me: See the other side of the horizon, and there was (also) a very huge group. It was said to me: This is your Ummah, and amongst them there were seventy thousand persons who would be made to enter Paradise without rendering any account and without (suffering) any torment. He then stood up and went to his house. Then the people began to talk about the people who would be admitted to Paradise without rendering any account and without (suffering) any torment. Some of them said: They may be those who (have had the good fortune of living) in the company of the Mes-senger of Allah (may peace be upon him) and some of them said: They be those who were born in Is-lam and did not associate anything with Allah. Some people mentioned other things.  Thereupon came forth the Messenger of Allah (may peace be upon him) before them and he said: What was that which you were talking about? They informed him. He said: They are those persons who neither practise charm, nor ask others to practise it, nor do they take omens, and repose their trust in their Lord. Upon this 'Ukkasha b. Mihsan stood up and said: Supplicate for me that He should make me one among them. Upon this he (Messenger of Allah) said: Thou are one among them. Then another man stood up and said: Supplicate before Allah that He should make me one among them. Upon this he said: 'Ukkisha has preceded you.
my Lord, the punishment of a tre-mendous Day.”
